title: Trailing degradation derivatives instead of static tyre thresholds
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent dynamic process with pit stops as state resets, and pit-stop DL work reports strong gains from temporal dependencies.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent dynamic process with pit stops as state resets, and pit-stop DL work reports strong gains from temporal dependencies. The current winning features are mostly levels and ratios, not causal slope/curvature of deterioration.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year, Driver, Stint`, add trailing 2-5 lap slope, curvature, EWMA, and change-point features for `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `TyreLife`; optionally estimate a simple Kalman/state-space latent pace-loss feature reset at pits.
expected_effect: Should detect tyre-cliff onset and separate sustained degradation from one-off noisy laps, especially in ambiguous mid/late-stint cases.
risk: Short stints and neutralized laps make derivatives noisy; all windows must be strictly causal and robust to extreme lap-time outliers.
